label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the question carefully. It asks us to compare the new device (the knee device) with Larry Rome's device (the bag) based on the provided information. First, let's identify the key information in the passage about both devices. The new knee device: - Connects to a person's knee. - Captures energy when the person slows down while walking. - Helps with the slowing down movement of the leg. - Produces electricity from the movement. - An adult walking quickly could produce 13 watts in a minute. - The device weighs about 1.5 kg, but is too costly. - It doesn't require carrying additional loads. Larry Rome's device: - A bag carried on a person's back. - Also produces power from walking. - Requires the walker to carry a load of 20 to 30 kg. - Produces more electricity than the knee device. Now let's analyze each option: (A) produces power without adding more loads to the walker: The knee device is attached to the knee and doesn't require carrying additional loads (unlike the bag, which requires carrying 20-30 kg). So this is true for the knee device. (B) can produce more power in a much shorter time: The passage says the knee device produces 13 watts in a minute. But it also says the bag produces more electricity than the knee device. So the knee device doesn't produce more power in a shorter time. This is false. (C) needs to be equipped with a battery: There's no mention of the need for a battery for either device in the passage. So we can't
